title: A Friendship That Grew from Being Neighbors

all.en: When my family first moved into our apartment building, an elderly woman named Mrs. Chen lived next door.
all.zh: 當我們家剛搬進這棟公寓時，隔壁住著一位名叫陳太太的年長女士。

all.en: On our first day, she knocked on our door with a plate of warm dumplings and a kind smile.
all.zh: 在我們搬來的第一天，她帶著一盤熱騰騰的水餃和親切的微笑來敲我們的門。

all.en: At first, I was just polite to her whenever we met in the hallway or elevator.
all.zh: 起初，我只是在走廊或電梯遇到她時對她保持禮貌。

all.en: However, one rainy afternoon, I helped her carry heavy grocery bags up three flights of stairs.
all.zh: 然而，在一個下雨的下午，我幫她把沉重的雜貨袋搬上三層樓的樓梯。

all.en: To thank me, she invited me in for tea and homemade pineapple cakes.
all.zh: 為了感謝我，她邀請我進去喝茶，並品嚐自製的鳳梨酥。

all.en: Her tiny living room was filled with old photographs and beautiful Chinese paintings.
all.zh: 她小小的客廳裡擺滿了舊照片和美麗的中國畫。

all.en: She told me stories about her youth, her late husband, and the city decades ago.
all.zh: 她跟我說了她年輕時的故事、她已故的丈夫，以及幾十年前的這座城市。

all.en: In return, I taught her how to use video calls so she could see her grandchildren overseas.
all.zh: 作為回報，我教她如何使用視訊通話，這樣她就能看到在海外的孫子們。

all.en: Soon, we developed a routine of having Sunday lunch together every weekend.
all.zh: 很快地，我們養成了每個週末一起吃星期天午餐的習慣。

all.en: She became like a grandmother to me, full of wisdom, humor, and gentle advice.
all.zh: 她對我來說就像一位祖母，充滿智慧、幽默和溫柔的建議。

all.en: Our unlikely friendship reminded me that family is not always defined by blood.
all.zh: 我們這段意想不到的友誼提醒了我，家人並非總是由血緣定義的。

all.en: Last winter, Mrs. Chen caught a bad cold and had to stay in bed for a whole week.
all.zh: 去年冬天，陳太太得了重感冒，必須臥床整整一週。

all.en: My mother cooked a pot of ginger chicken soup and asked me to bring it next door.
all.zh: 我媽媽煮了一鍋薑汁雞湯，要我送到隔壁去。

all.en: Mrs. Chen was so touched that she insisted on writing a thank-you card by hand.
all.zh: 陳太太非常感動，堅持要親手寫一張感謝卡。

all.en: After she recovered, she taught my mother how to make her famous radish cake.
all.zh: 她康復後，教我媽媽做她著名的蘿蔔糕。

all.en: The two of them now spend every Lunar New Year preparing dishes side by side.
all.zh: 她們倆現在每個農曆新年都並肩準備料理。

all.en: Her grandchildren finally visited from abroad and stayed in our building for a week.
all.zh: 她的孫子們終於從國外回來探望，並在我們的大樓住了一週。

all.en: They thanked me with a small wooden gift for taking such good care of their grandma.
all.zh: 他們以一個小小的木製禮物感謝我，謝謝我把他們的奶奶照顧得這麼好。

all.en: On her eightieth birthday, our whole floor gathered for a quiet candlelit celebration.
all.zh: 在她八十歲生日那天，我們整層樓的鄰居為她舉辦了一場安靜的燭光慶祝會。

all.en: She held my hand tightly and said that meeting our family was a true blessing.
all.zh: 她緊緊握住我的手，說認識我們一家是她真正的福氣。

all.en: Years from now, I will still remember how a plate of dumplings began everything.
all.zh: 多年以後，我仍會記得一切是如何從一盤水餃開始的。

all.en: The spring after her birthday, Mrs. Chen began teaching me her old family calligraphy style.
all.zh: 她生日後的那個春天，陳太太開始教我她家傳的書法風格。

all.en: Every Tuesday evening, I sat at her wooden table practicing strokes with a soft brush.
all.zh: 每個星期二晚上，我都坐在她的木桌前，用軟毛筆練習筆畫。

all.en: She patiently corrected my hand position and shared stories about her late father's lessons.
all.zh: 她耐心地調整我的執筆姿勢，並分享她已故父親當年教她的故事。

all.en: In return, I helped her digitize old letters and photographs onto a small laptop.
all.zh: 作為回報，我幫她把舊信件和照片數位化，儲存到一台小筆電裡。

all.en: Her grandchildren overseas were thrilled to receive scanned copies of family treasures.
all.zh: 她在海外的孫子們收到家族珍寶的掃描檔案時，都欣喜萬分。

all.en: One quiet Sunday, Mrs. Chen gave me her oldest calligraphy brush as a special gift.
all.zh: 在一個寧靜的星期天，陳太太把她最古老的書法毛筆送給我，作為一份特別的禮物。

all.en: She told me it had belonged to her father and now it should belong to me.
all.zh: 她告訴我那曾屬於她父親，如今應該屬於我。

all.en: I framed one of her handwritten poems and hung it proudly above my bedroom desk.
all.zh: 我把她親筆寫的一首詩裱框，驕傲地掛在我臥房書桌的上方。

all.en: When she passed away peacefully the following winter, our entire floor mourned together quietly.
all.zh: 當她在隔年冬天安詳離世時，我們整層樓的鄰居一起靜靜地哀悼。

all.en: Every dumpling I cook now carries the warmth of the neighbor who taught me family.
all.zh: 如今我煮的每一顆水餃，都帶著那位教我何謂家人的鄰居所傳遞的溫暖。

all.en: After Mrs. Chen passed away, her family asked me to keep a small box of her belongings.
all.zh: 陳太太過世後，她的家人請我保管一個裝著她遺物的小盒子。

all.en: Inside, I found her handwritten recipes, faded letters, and a tiny notebook of poems.
all.zh: 裡頭有她手寫的食譜、泛黃的書信，還有一本寫滿詩的小筆記本。

all.en: Her grandchildren visited that spring and asked if I could continue their grandmother's traditions.
all.zh: 那年春天她的孫子們前來探望，問我能否延續他們奶奶的傳統。

all.en: Together, we hosted a small dumpling-making afternoon for every neighbor on our floor.
all.zh: 我們一起為整層樓的鄰居舉辦了一場小小的水餃製作下午聚會。

all.en: I taught the children how to fold dumplings exactly the way Mrs. Chen had once shown me.
all.zh: 我教孩子們如何包水餃，正如陳太太當年教我那樣。

all.en: Their laughter filled the kitchen and brought a familiar warmth back to her quiet apartment.
all.zh: 他們的笑聲充滿了廚房，把熟悉的溫暖帶回她那寧靜的公寓。

all.en: My mother began teaching her radish cake recipe to a young couple who had just moved in.
all.zh: 我媽媽開始把她的蘿蔔糕食譜教給剛搬進來的一對年輕夫妻。

all.en: The building organized a small annual gathering in Mrs. Chen's honor every Lunar New Year.
all.zh: 大樓每年農曆新年都會舉辦一場小型聚會，以紀念陳太太。

all.en: I framed one of her poems and gave copies to every neighbor who had once known her smile.
all.zh: 我把她其中一首詩裱了框，並把副本送給每一位曾認識她笑容的鄰居。

all.en: A single plate of dumplings, I learned, can quietly shape a whole community for many years.
all.zh: 我學到，一盤水餃竟能在許多年裡靜靜地形塑一整個社群。

all.en: Years after the building's first Lunar New Year gathering, our community grew warmer with each passing season.
all.zh: 大樓的第一場農曆新年聚會多年之後，我們的社群在每一個流轉的季節中變得更加溫暖。

all.en: Mrs. Chen's grandchildren returned every summer and stayed with various neighbors throughout our whole building.
all.zh: 陳太太的孫子們每年夏天都會回來，並輪流住在我們整棟大樓不同鄰居的家中。

all.en: They learned to cook radish cake, fold dumplings, and write calligraphy from many quiet, patient hands.
all.zh: 他們從許多安靜耐心的雙手中，學會了煮蘿蔔糕、包水餃和寫書法。

all.en: On weekends, the youngest grandchild sat beside me at the wooden table, practicing the same brushstrokes.
all.zh: 在週末，最小的孫子會坐在我身邊，在那張木桌前練習同樣的筆畫。

all.en: Her handwriting slowly grew steadier, just as mine had under Mrs. Chen's gentle quiet guidance.
all.zh: 她的字跡漸漸變得穩定，就如同我當年在陳太太溫柔安靜的指導下一樣。

all.en: I framed her first complete poem and sent it overseas to thank her parents for their trust.
all.zh: 我把她第一首完成的詩裱了框，寄到海外去，感謝她父母對我的信任。

all.en: Our annual gathering eventually welcomed neighbors from three nearby buildings on our small quiet street.
all.zh: 我們的年度聚會後來也歡迎了我們小小寧靜街道上鄰近三棟大樓的鄰居。

all.en: Children of ten different families now share recipes that originally belonged to one kind old woman.
all.zh: 十個不同家庭的孩子如今共享著原本只屬於一位善良老婦人的食譜。

all.en: Every Lunar New Year, our hallway smells of dumplings, radish cake, and warm shared laughter together.
all.zh: 每年農曆新年，我們的走廊都瀰漫著水餃、蘿蔔糕的香味，以及一起分享的溫暖笑聲。

all.en: A single plate of warm dumplings, I now understand, can quietly raise an entire neighborhood across generations.
all.zh: 我如今明白，一盤溫熱的水餃，能跨越世代靜靜地養育起一整個社區。

all.en: In her honor, our building installed a small wooden noticeboard near the elevator on the ground floor.
all.zh: 為紀念她，我們的大樓在一樓電梯附近設置了一塊小小的木製公告欄。

all.en: Neighbors began pinning handwritten recipes, kind notes, and gentle reminders about shared community events.
all.zh: 鄰居們開始在上面釘上手寫的食譜、親切的小紙條，以及社區共同活動的溫柔提醒。

all.en: Each month, one family hosts an evening of stories about their hometown and favorite local dishes.
all.zh: 每個月，會有一個家庭主辦一場夜晚活動，分享他們家鄉的故事和最愛的家常菜。

all.en: Mrs. Chen's youngest grandchild, now a quiet teenager, recently moved back to attend high school here.
all.zh: 陳太太最小的孫子如今已是個安靜的青少年，最近搬回來在這裡讀高中。

all.en: She lives in her grandmother's old apartment, surrounded by familiar paintings and the same wooden table.
all.zh: 她住在祖母昔日的公寓裡，身邊圍繞著熟悉的畫作和那張同樣的木桌。

all.en: Every Sunday she joins my family for lunch, just as her grandmother once did long ago.
all.zh: 每個星期天她都來我家共進午餐，就像她祖母多年前的習慣一樣。

all.en: She brings the calligraphy brush her grandmother gave me, and we practice together at the wooden table.
all.zh: 她帶來她祖母當年送我的那支毛筆，我們在木桌前一起練字。

all.en: Her handwriting now resembles Mrs. Chen's so closely that my mother often tears up gently.
all.zh: 她的字跡如今神似陳太太，我媽媽常常為此眼眶輕輕泛淚。

all.en: Last Lunar New Year, we welcomed eighty neighbors to our gathering across four nearby buildings together.
all.zh: 去年農曆新年，我們在四棟鄰近大樓中迎接了八十位鄰居一同聚會。

all.en: A single kind woman, gone but never forgotten, had quietly knit our entire small street into one family.
all.zh: 一位善良的女士雖已離去卻從未被遺忘，她悄悄地把我們整條小街織成了一家人。

all.en: This spring, Mrs. Chen's granddaughter graduated from high school with top marks in literature and history.
all.zh: 今年春天，陳太太的孫女以文學和歷史的優異成績從高中畢業。

all.en: Our whole hallway gathered to celebrate, bringing handmade dumplings and warm congratulations from every floor.
all.zh: 我們整條走廊的人都齊聚一堂慶祝，從各個樓層帶來手做的水餃和溫暖的祝福。

all.en: She chose to study Chinese calligraphy and classical poetry at a quiet university in her grandmother's hometown.
all.zh: 她選擇到祖母家鄉那所安靜的大學主修中國書法和古典詩詞。

all.en: Before leaving, she gifted my mother a small handwritten poem describing our hallway as her second family.
all.zh: 離開前，她送給我媽媽一首小小的手寫詩，描述我們的走廊是她的第二個家。

all.en: She promised to return for every Lunar New Year and continue teaching the youngest children calligraphy basics.
all.zh: 她承諾每年農曆新年都回來，並繼續教最小的孩子們書法的基礎。

all.en: After she left, neighbors took turns hosting small Sunday lunches to keep the warm tradition alive.
all.zh: 她離開後，鄰居們輪流主辦小型的週日午餐，讓這份溫暖的傳統持續下去。

all.en: A new family from another country moved into our building and were welcomed with dumplings on day one.
all.zh: 一個來自另一個國家的新家庭搬進我們大樓，第一天就受到水餃的款待。

all.en: Their young daughter quickly learned to fold dumplings and helped pin recipes onto the wooden noticeboard.
all.zh: 他們的小女兒很快學會了包水餃，並幫忙把食譜釘上那塊木製公告欄。

all.en: My mother began writing a small handwritten cookbook gathering every neighbor's favorite shared family recipe.
all.zh: 我媽媽開始手寫一本小小的食譜，收集每位鄰居最愛的家傳料理。

all.en: On the cover, she carefully wrote one short line: dedicated to Mrs. Chen, who began everything quietly.
all.zh: 在封面上，她仔細地寫下一句話：獻給陳太太，是她安靜地開啟了這一切。
